payloads.text:
You‚Äôre super close ‚Äî screenshot confirms exactly what happened:

- You set `allowedOrigins` as a **string**, not an array.
- That‚Äôs why it says: `expected array, received string`.

### Fastest unblock (do this)
Use fallback mode instead of array typing:

```powershell
openclaw config set gateway.controlUi.dangerouslyAllowHostHeaderOriginFallback true
openclaw gateway restart
openclaw status --deep
```

That should let non-loopback LAN bind work without wrestling JSON array syntax.

---

### If you want strict mode (proper array), do it via file edit:
```powershell
notepad $env:USERPROFILE\.openclaw\openclaw.json
```
Set this exactly:
```json
"gateway": {
"controlUi": {
"allowedOrigins": [
"http://192.168.1.144:18790",
"http://localhost:18790",
"http://127.0.0.1:18790"
]
}
}
```
Save, then:
```powershell
openclaw gateway restart
```

If you want, do the first 3-command fallback now and paste output ‚Äî I‚Äôll verify immediately.
